metadata.comment: Baseline design synthesized with Cadence Genus.
The first netlist generator produced by the agent
Replaced decomposed carry and prefix merge logic with MAJx2 and AO21x2 cells while preserving the structural MAC topology.
Reworked the generated candidate to use a Dadda-style reduction schedule, then downsized non-critical prefix/output cells to AND2x2/AO21x1/BUFx2 while keeping the design fully structural.
Promoted worker-round-1 after removing the explicit output buffer stage and driving D directly from the final sum network.
Promoted worker-round-3 after downsizing only the compression-tree XOR instances to XOR2xp5 while preserving the faster XOR2x2 cells in the prefix and final sum network.
Promoted worker-round-4 after downsizing the final output-sum XOR stage from XOR2x2 to XOR2xp5 without changing the compression tree or prefix network.
Promoted worker-dadda-connect after reordering Dadda compressor scheduling by estimated arrival time so later-arriving signals pass through fewer FA sum chains.
Promoted worker-cell-sizing after keeping prefix XOR cells near the critical window at XOR2x2 while downsizing the remaining non-critical prefix XOR cells to XOR2xp5.
Promoted worker-polarity-1 after rewriting the final prefix stage to use inverted carry generation with AOI21xp5 and XNOR2xp5-based output summation.
Promoted worker-polarity-3 after using mixed-polarity compressor templates in low non-critical Dadda columns, keeping sum/carry inverted until a positive form was actually needed.
Promoted worker-final-polarity after expanding polarity-aware handling across the survivor-to-prefix boundary and extending mixed-polarity compression into the high final-Dadda region.
Promoted autonomous worker round auto05 after expanding the high mixed-polarity compression window to columns 18..31 without degrading timing.
Promoted worker-midwin-2 under timing-priority criteria after speeding up the compression-tree XOR chain that feeds the D18 critical window.
Promoted worker-balance-2 after refining the D18 compression fast window asymmetrically, keeping only the second XOR stage of the critical compression chain on the faster cell.

Promoted worker-claude-b1 after rewriting the top output bit to keep its local parity in the complemented domain until the final XOR, eliminating unnecessary high-bit carry/polarity restoration logic.
Promoted worker-lib-aoi21 after strengthening only the top-bit final carry-bar AOI21 stage, shifting the worst path from D31 to D30 without area regression.

Observed the D31 high-bit final-adder carry chain as the bottleneck in the ripple-style prefix stage; replaced the final adder lowering with a reusable Kogge-Stone prefix network plus explicit logic/asap7 support; this changed the candidate from timing fail to timing pass with much lower critical delay at the cost of higher area.

Observed the high-bit prefix generate chain as the remaining bottleneck after the Kogge-Stone transition; replaced two-level generate/carry logic with reusable AO21-based lowering across the prefix tree and full-adder carry path; this improved timing and reduced area by shifting the worst path away from the former D31 prefix tail.

Observed the compressor-tail to prefix-entry path as the new bottleneck after the AO21-based prefix carry refactor; introduced an arrival-aware Dadda compressor scheduler that keeps later-arriving signals off longer xor_sum chains and prefers them on the faster cin/carry path; this improved timing without any area or cell-count regression.

Observed that arith-to-logic lowering flattened away module hierarchy; replaced each arith block with a private func.func-backed logic region plus top-level hierarchical instances, and encoded logic.region_kind so future patterns can target only compressor_tree or prefix_tree regions; this preserved correctness and QoR while establishing reusable region-scoped rewrite structure.

Observed that the new hierarchical logic regions had no downstream optimization using logic.region_kind; added a reusable region-scoped ASAP7 cell sizing pass that reclaims area in non-critical compressor_tree and prefix_tree regions while preserving the current timing window; this validated the new region structure and reduced area without timing or cell-count regression.

Observed that region-scoped area reclaim still lacked explicit timing-derived instance guidance and that candidate simulation failed because Icarus could not compile ASAP7 specify blocks; added reusable func-level timing annotations for both keep-fast and reclaim instance sets, drove region-scoped cell sizing from those annotations, and sanitized stdcell models in the sim wrapper; this preserved correctness and timing pass, improved area from 169.0 to 164.0, and restored make sim at the cost of a small critical_delay regression from 0.6279 to 0.6347.

Observed that the timing analyzer still treated preserved module boundaries too locally and could misclassify compressor and prefix instances near inter-module critical windows; extended the reusable func timing analysis to propagate top-level input arrival and downstream tail information across the instance graph, then used those bidirectional scores to preserve globally critical cells; this kept correctness and timing pass, improved critical_delay from 0.6347 to 0.6283 and wns from 0.2653 to 0.2717, and increased area from 164.0 to 166.0.

Observed that the compressor-tree critical path still lacked a global-design-aware local rewrite stage; added a reusable ASAP7-IR timing-guided compressor optimization pass that iteratively rebinds the latest-arriving full-adder input onto cin using whole-design arrival and tail analysis, then searched 0/1/2/3/4/8 iteration budgets and selected 3 as the best efficiency-QoR tradeoff; this preserved correctness and area while improving critical_delay from 0.6283 to 0.6275 and wns from 0.2717 to 0.2725.
